source: ratified frozen briefing (evals/replay/thin-sam/expected.json) — seeded offline, no live call

prose.headline: Sam gave only one-word answers all session — this is a partial read, not a verdict on pace, clarity, or engagement.
summary_bullets: Nine straight turns stayed at "fine," "ok," "i guess," "not sure," and "dunno" — the record is too thin to read what landed for Sam.
The only clear signal is the silence itself: no friction, priorities, or lift got named, so the check-in never got past surface level.
prose.understanding_paragraph: We did not learn what actually landed for Sam, what snagged, or what she wants next from this fortnight. The session showed a non-functional read: every turn stayed at one or two words, so there is no reliable story beyond the lack of detail.
prose.brutal_truth_employee: Too few turns hold a real note on Sam to call this a read. What we do have is a wall of "fine," "ok," "i guess," "not sure," and "dunno," which says nothing beyond Sam not opening up here.
prose.brutal_truth_manager: You kept moving past "i guess" and "not sure" instead of stopping to pin down one concrete example. The turn after "dunno" was the moment to switch from broad check-in to a specific prompt about one thing that landed or snagged.

next_actions.action: Re-ask the landing question with one concrete example prompt: "what shipped, what didn't, and what was the one snag?"
Set up a shorter follow-up with a tighter prompt list and no broad opener.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: if Sam stays at one-word answers again, treat this as a conversation problem, not a work update.
Within two weeks: ask for one specific thing that landed and one thing that snagged; if neither comes, the read is still missing.
